title: 'No real sense of direction': EU struggling to find path to sanctioning Israeli settlements
content: EU ambassadors have failed to agree on the next steps for sanctioning Israeli settlements – and with no majority in favour of any option, diplomats have admitted there's no clear way forward.
snippet: EU ambassadors have failed to agree on the next steps for sanctioning Israeli settlements – and with no majority in favour of any option, diplomats have admitted there's no clear way forward.

title: One dead and four injured after Czech military helicopter crashes at air base
content: An investigation into the incident is underway. All of the military's H-1 helicopters have been grounded until it is completed, the Czech armed forces said in a statement.
snippet: An investigation into the incident is underway. All of the military's H-1 helicopters have been grounded until it is completed, the Czech armed forces said in a statement.

title: Fact check: No evidence links Norway fire to alleged Israeli settlement import ban
content: A massive fire in Norway has destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate. As the blaze spread, posts on social media began linking it to claims that Oslo had banned imports of Israeli settlement products. The Cube investigates.

title: Lavrov tells Rubio 'unacceptable' for US to continue arms sales to Kyiv

title: Brussels slaps €890 million fine on Google despite looming new US tariffs
content: Brussels fined Google €890 million under its Big Tech rulebook for favouring its own services in search results and restricting app developers, despite Washington preparing a fresh set of trade tariffs as current ones are expiring.

title: Germany's Europe minister on EU budget: 'Options are limited'
content: For senior German politician Gunther Krichbaum (CDU), Minister of State for Europe at the Foreign Office, the EU must now rethink and break new ground, both on enlargement and on the budget.
snippet: For senior German politician Gunther Krichbaum (CDU), Minister of State for Europe at the Foreign Office, the EU must now rethink and break new ground, both on enlargement and on the budget.

title: Euronews in Cotignac: How a historic French village survived a night of wildfire
content: Euronews reports on the ground in France, where a blaze tore through a medieval village in Provence overnight, destroying dozens of homes and burning thousands of hectares, as residents opened their doors to strangers.

title: Could Gianni Infantino really become UN Secretary General if Trump puts him forward?
content: US President can push FIFA’s boss to lead the United Nations, but not impose him over the other candidates – and the other permanent members of the Security Council would need to endorse him.
snippet: US President can push FIFA’s boss to lead the United Nations, but not impose him over the other candidates – and the other permanent members of the Security Council would need to endorse him.

title: Former UK water company CEO charged over alleged water test fraud
content: Under UK law, intentionally altering wastewater flows during regulatory sampling is a criminal offence. If convicted, Wright faces a maximum sentence of 10 years in prison.
snippet: Under UK law, intentionally altering wastewater flows during regulatory sampling is a criminal offence. If convicted, Wright faces a maximum sentence of 10 years in prison.

title: Zelenskyy replaces Oleksandr Syrskyi with Mykhailo Drapatyi as Ukraine's commander-in-chief
content: Under pressure from civil society and widespread protests across Ukraine, President Zelenskyy on Tuesday replaced General Syrskyi with Mykhailo Drapatyi as Ukraine's commander-in-chief.
snippet: Under pressure from civil society and widespread protests across Ukraine, President Zelenskyy on Tuesday replaced General Syrskyi with Mykhailo Drapatyi as Ukraine's commander-in-chief.

title: Spanish singer Julio Iglesias sues Spain's deputy PM Yolanda Díaz, demands €50,000
content: The singer has filed a lawsuit in the Supreme Court against the deputy prime minister, alleging a violation of his right to honour and seeking €50,000 over remarks she made about sexual abuse allegations brought by two former employees.
snippet: The singer has filed a lawsuit in the Supreme Court against the deputy prime minister, alleging a violation of his right to honour and seeking €50,000 over remarks she made about sexual abuse

title: France becomes the first country in Europe to officially ban social media for under-15s

snippet: Other EU countries such as Greece, Slovenia and Sweden are planning to restrict minors' access to social media.

title: Brussels prepares first digital rulebook fine against Google, risking Trump's ire: media reports
content: German media reports say the EU executive is set to announce the fine against the tech titan on Thursday in a move that could escalate tensions with the Trump administration over digital services.
snippet: German media reports say the EU executive is set to announce the fine against the tech titan on Thursday in a move that could escalate tensions with the Trump administration over digital services.

title: UK to remove 5% tax on electricity bills as Burnham holds first cabinet meeting
content: The UK's new prime minister, Andy Burnham, has held his first cabinet meeting, telling ministers he will set out his plans "over the coming days" and promised to reveal how his government will pay for any reductions in tax.

title: An estimated two million fans fill Madrid’s streets to welcome world champions

snippet: Spain's national team marks a historic day of World Cup celebrations on Monday. The champions will tour Madrid in an open-top bus before the final celebration.

title: Chess legend Judit Polgar rejects offer to become Hungary's president
content: Until a successor is elected, the Speaker of Parliament, Agnes Forsthoffer, is serving as interim head of state. Parliament must elect a new president within 30 days.
snippet: Until a successor is elected, the Speaker of Parliament, Agnes Forsthoffer, is serving as interim head of state. Parliament must elect a new president within 30 days.

title: Iceland has 'nothing to lose' in EU membership talks, says French foreign minister
content: Iceland first applied to join the European Union in 2009, in the wake of the collapse of its financial sector, but suspended accession talks in 2015.
snippet: Iceland first applied to join the European Union in 2009, in the wake of the collapse of its financial sector, but suspended accession talks in 2015.

title: Attacks on Ukrainians in Poland rise, police data show
content: Police received 180 reports of alleged hate crimes against Ukrainian citizens in the first half of the year, an increase of more than 30%.
snippet: Police received 180 reports of alleged hate crimes against Ukrainian citizens in the first half of the year, an increase of more than 30%.

title: Former Liverpool and England star Kevin Keegan dies aged 75
content: Keegan also wrote newspaper columns, became one of the first English footballers to have his own branded football boots and released the hit single Head Over Heels in Love.
snippet: Keegan also wrote newspaper columns, became one of the first English footballers to have his own branded football boots and released the hit single Head Over Heels in Love.

title: Man jailed for life over terror plot targeting UK military academy
content: Bashir left the UK two days after the synagogue attack and was arrested at Manchester Airport in November 2025 when he returned from a family wedding in Pakistan.
snippet: Bashir left the UK two days after the synagogue attack and was arrested at Manchester Airport in November 2025 when he returned from a family wedding in Pakistan.

title: EU temporarily extends controversial chat-scanning regime until 2028
content: EU countries have adopted a temporary regime allowing messaging services to voluntarily detect child sexual abuse material until 2028. End-to-end encrypted apps like WhatsApp and Signal remain exempt.
snippet: EU countries have adopted a temporary regime allowing messaging services to voluntarily detect child sexual abuse material until 2028. End-to-end encrypted apps like WhatsApp and Signal remain exempt.

title: Far-right activists to sue European Commission over 'remigration' rejection
content: The European Commission has rejected a European Citizens' Initiative seeking to suspend asylum and family reunification and return "unintegrated" non-European migrants to their countries of origin. Its organisers are preparing a legal challenge.
snippet: The European Commission has rejected a European Citizens' Initiative seeking to suspend asylum and family reunification and return "unintegrated" non-European migrants to their countries of origin.

title: "NATO will defend Bulgaria against any action by Iran," official warns
content: Iran has threatened Bulgaria after the government sit aid plans to host US military aircraft. Tehran warned Sofia must not allow its territory to become a launchpad for operations against Tehran. Colonel Martin O'Donnell tells Euronews NATO is ready to defend Bulgaria should it come under attack.
snippet: Iran has threatened Bulgaria after the government sit aid plans to host US military aircraft. Tehran warned Sofia must not allow its territory to become a launchpad for operations against Tehran.

title: Putin stepping up hybrid war on Latvia because he’s ‘losing in Ukraine’, PM Kulbergs says

snippet: In an exclusive interview with Europe Today, Latvia’s Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs says the “top level of the Russian mob” is behind a recent uptick in attempted migrant crossings into his country

title: EU agrees new sanctions against Russia as Greece secures LNG exemption

snippet: The deal reached by ambassadors grants Greece an exemption to continue shipping Russian LNG to non-EU clients for the foreseeable future.

title: Green homes: Which countries are making their buildings more energy efficient?

title: Air passenger rights: This is how you get your money back
content: The European Council approved the agreement on stronger air passenger rights on 13 July, following MEPs’ backing on 7 July. The new rules come as passengers struggle to fully exercise their rights, leaving airlines with €3.2 billion in unpaid compensation.

content: Andris Kulbergs became Latvia's Prime Minister in May after the previous coalition government collapsed over its handling of stray drones. title: Hungary's prosecutor resigns under pressure in political win for Magyar
content: Hungary's Prosecutor General, Gábor Bálint Nagy, has resigned amid mounting political pressure as Prime Minister Péter Magyar seeks to replace Orbán-era officials. The move follows allegations of political bias by lawyers for detained Ukrainian cash couriers in a cross-border cash seizure case.

title: Is Europe's new Entry/Exit system changing how Britons travel?

title: Fact check: Is the EU set to ban bottled water multipacks?
